originalAlarm.description: Potential leak of memory pointed to by '__ptr_13'

sanitizedDescription: Potential leak of memory pointed to by 'ptr'
reasoning: 6e2b75740be.c L39: Memory leak of `ptr` — allocated at L37 (`ptr = malloc(512)`) and stored in module_core(mod) at L43, but on the success path (return 0 at L56), ptr/module_core(mod) is never freed. New bug (no ERROR; the known bugs are about free-after-free and uninitialized access). Leak occurs in all configurations since the malloc and the success-path return are unconditionally executed (no #ifdef guard). PC = true. Correctly constrained.
